description: A family of five received a 3-day pay-or-quit notice after losing income during a medical emergency. The landlord had previously accepted late payments without notice for 18 months. The lease did not specify a late fee provision.
outcome_notes: Argued that landlord's prior course of dealing waived strict enforcement of the payment deadline. Court granted a 30-day cure period. Family retained housing.

description: Single mother with two minor children facing eviction for alleged lease violation after hosting her sister for 6 weeks. Landlord claimed unauthorized occupant. Lease permitted guests up to 30 days.
outcome_notes: Argued 6-week stay did not convert guest to occupant under state tenancy law. Lease cure provision required 10-day notice before eviction filing. Case dismissed.

description: Elderly tenant of 14 years received a no-fault eviction notice citing owner move-in. Landlord failed to file required relocation assistance paperwork within the mandated 30-day window.
outcome_notes: Procedural defect in owner move-in filing invalidated the eviction notice. Tenant received 6-month extension and full relocation assistance per city ordinance.

description: Tenant received a 5-day notice for nonpayment after a bank error caused the rent check to bounce. Landlord refused to accept re-payment and filed for eviction within 48 hours.
outcome_notes: Bank error documented by written statement. Rent tendered in full before court date. Judge dismissed for lack of good cause given immediate cure attempt.

description: Tenant with documented disability requested reasonable accommodation for a support animal. Landlord denied the request citing a no-pets policy and issued eviction notice when the animal was not removed.
outcome_notes: Fair Housing Act requires reasonable accommodation for support animals regardless of no-pets policy. Eviction stayed pending accommodation review. Animal permitted, case closed.

description: Month-to-month tenant in a rent-controlled unit received a rent increase of 34% followed by eviction notice when the increase was rejected. Local ordinance capped annual increases at 5%.
outcome_notes: Landlord agreed to rescind the increase and limit rent to allowed cap in exchange for tenant waiving past-due claims. Tenant remained housed.

description: Tenant alleged habitability defects including a broken heating system for three winter months. Landlord retaliated with an eviction notice two weeks after tenant filed a code complaint.
outcome_notes: Retaliatory eviction defense raised under state statute. Settled with landlord making all repairs, waiving two months rent, and withdrawing eviction.

description: Commercial tenant operating a small restaurant was evicted after landlord claimed lease breach due to exterior signage. Tenant argued signage had been in place unchanged for 8 years without objection.
outcome_notes: Lease explicitly prohibited exterior modifications without written consent. Long-standing acceptance did not create an easement under commercial tenancy law. Case not taken.

description: Long-term resident of 22 years with two US-citizen children received a final order of removal after missing a hearing date due to a mail delivery failure. A prior attorney had filed a defective change-of-address form.
outcome_notes: Filed motion to reopen based on ineffective assistance of prior counsel and lack of notice. Immigration court granted reopening. Client placed in withholding-only proceedings.

description: Asylum seeker from a country with active political persecution had their case denied after a one-year filing bar was triggered by an administrative delay at the resettlement agency.
outcome_notes: Documented that agency delay — not client inaction — caused the filing bar violation. Extraordinary circumstances exception granted. Case reopened and approved.

description: DACA recipient whose renewal was denied due to a background check error involving a dismissed misdemeanor charge. USCIS treated the dismissal as a conviction in error.
outcome_notes: Submitted certified court disposition showing charge was dismissed without conviction. USCIS conceded error and granted DACA renewal on motion for reconsideration.

description: Undocumented parent of three minor children detained during a routine check-in. Family had been compliant for 7 years with regular appearances and had a pending petition.
outcome_notes: Filed emergency stay of removal citing pending I-130 and humanitarian factors. Detained parent released on order of supervision pending adjudication of petition.

description: Client with T visa eligibility due to trafficking victimization was denied certification by local law enforcement who refused to cooperate with the process.
outcome_notes: Escalated to state attorney general's office. Law enforcement issued certification under pressure from state oversight. T visa application filed successfully.

description: Refugee family with approved resettlement status lost their housing and benefits due to a data entry error in the state refugee office system that marked them as non-compliant.
outcome_notes: Administrative error corrected after advocacy with state refugee coordinator. Benefits restored with back payment. Family re-housed within 30 days.

description: Client seeking cancellation of removal based on 10-year continuous presence had their case undermined by a prior attorney who failed to file timely and correct documentation.
outcome_notes: Continuous presence clock was legally stopped by a prior NTA that predated our involvement. Cancellation eligibility not established. Case not taken.

description: Restaurant worker classified as an independent contractor was denied overtime pay and tips pooled illegally with management-level employees. Worker had no written contract and was scheduled by employer.
outcome_notes: Applied economic reality test — employer controlled hours, tools, and pricing. Worker reclassified as employee. Recovered 2 years of unpaid overtime plus tip pool violations.

description: Domestic worker employed full-time for four years was paid below minimum wage and denied any overtime despite regularly working 55-hour weeks. No pay stubs were provided.
outcome_notes: Reconstructed hours from text messages and employer calendar. Domestic workers covered under state wage law. Recovered $38,000 in back wages plus liquidated damages.

description: Farmworker crew paid piece-rate wages that fell below minimum wage in weeks with poor harvest conditions. Employer failed to separately compensate rest periods as required by state law.
outcome_notes: State labor commissioner found violations on both minimum wage floor and rest period compensation. Penalty assessment plus back wages paid to 12 workers in crew.

description: Construction laborer was not paid final two weeks of wages after being terminated without cause. Employer claimed work was substandard and offset wages against alleged damages.
outcome_notes: State law prohibits offset of wages against unliquidated tort claims. Final wages due immediately upon termination. Small claims recovery in full plus waiting-time penalties.

description: Nail salon worker paid a flat weekly salary of $300 regardless of hours worked. Some weeks involved 65-hour schedules. Employer claimed the flat rate covered all hours.
outcome_notes: Fluctuating-workweek doctrine does not apply when weekly rate falls below minimum wage floor. Settled for $22,000 covering 3 years of underpayment.

description: Hotel housekeeper had automatic meal break deductions applied to shifts where breaks were never taken due to staffing shortages. Employer policy did not enforce break compliance.
outcome_notes: Employer liability established for non-compliant auto-deduct policy. Settled for one premium pay hour per missed break day for 18-month period.

description: Delivery driver classified as an independent contractor filed complaint after vehicle expenses reduced net earnings below minimum wage. Employer set all delivery routes and rates.
outcome_notes: Misclassification likely under ABC test but litigation risk high. Negotiated settlement of $15,000 covering expense reimbursements and wage differential.

description: Warehouse worker claims 3 years of unpaid wages. Prior employer has dissolved the LLC and the principals have opened a successor company. No assets identified in dissolved entity.
outcome_notes: Successor liability analysis inconclusive. No direct evidence of fraudulent transfer. Case not taken due to low recovery probability against dissolved entity.

description: Father sought modification of custody after mother relocated 400 miles away without court approval, violating the existing parenting plan's geographic restriction clause.
outcome_notes: Relocation without notice and consent constituted a material change in circumstances. Court ordered return of child to original jurisdiction and modified custody to primary with father.

description: Mother filed emergency motion after father failed to return child from scheduled visitation for 72 hours and turned off his phone. Father later claimed miscommunication about schedule.
outcome_notes: Emergency pick-up order granted within 4 hours. Child returned safely. Family court found pattern of parenting plan violations. Supervised visitation imposed on father.

description: Grandparent petitioned for third-party visitation rights after parents denied all contact following an acrimonious divorce. Grandparent had been primary caregiver for 3 years.
outcome_notes: Mediation resulted in structured visitation schedule for grandparent. Parents retained decision-making authority. No court order needed — agreement signed.

description: Non-custodial parent filed to reduce child support after job loss. Custodial parent claims income underreporting and requested forensic accounting of new employer's pay structure.
outcome_notes: Income documentation produced showed genuine reduction. Support modified downward temporarily with automatic review trigger at 90 days.

description: Same-sex couple disputing custody after separation where one parent was biological and the other had no legal adoption in place. Non-biological parent sought de facto parent recognition.
outcome_notes: De facto parent status recognized under state equitable parentage doctrine given 5-year caregiving role. Joint custody arrangement negotiated in mediation.

description: Parent with history of domestic violence sought to regain unsupervised visitation. Opposing counsel argued DV findings from prior criminal case should preclude modification.
outcome_notes: Agreed to 6 months of professionally supervised visitation with reassessment. DV intervention program completion required before any further modification hearing.

description: Father filed for sole custody alleging mother's untreated substance abuse posed danger to child. Mother disputed the allegations and accused father of parental alienation.
outcome_notes: Both parties had credibility issues and contested factual record. Custody evaluation ordered by court. Declined to take case without clearer facts establishing imminent harm.
